description: Nikos golden set — derived from his actual food_log entries Apr 16-17, 2026. Each case reconstructs a plausible raw-text input a user would type, with expected macro ranges drawn from Nikos's logged entries (which our current local-DB resolves against USDA/Kavdas defaults). Tolerance bands account for serving-size ambiguity.

cases.notes: Canonical egg test. 1 large egg = 72 cal / 6.3g P per our rules.
Default serving assumption — was logged at 100g yielding 250/26/15.
Explicit 60g — tests whether parser honors explicit grams (Nikos's log showed 60g at 79cal which undercounts; likely a wrong-unit bug worth catching).

cases.input: palm of chicken breast, cup of cooked rice, and one banana
cases.notes: Multi-item; Kavdas 'palm' = ~120g chicken; 'cup' rice = 100-150g cooked; banana ~107cal.
Vague input; tests reasonable default (Nikos got 100cal/10g P — low end plausible).
Branded item, AI estimate acceptable; tests sugar_g estimation.

cases.input: 1 rice cake with 1 tablespoon of honey
cases.notes: Common Greek snack combo; tests compound parsing + honey sugar accuracy.
Tests 'handful' = 30g default per our serving-size rules.
Greek-language + Greek units (φλ=cup ~100-150g rice cooked, παλάμη=palm ~120g chicken).

cases.input: half an avocado, one tortilla wrap, and a scoop of whey protein
cases.notes: Complex compound, mixed food types; tests fractional qty (half), 'scoop' unit.
